Phase-45.NAME: First VPN Handshake Trial Activation and Subscription Calendar
Phase-45.purpose: Change KrotPN trial semantics so verified users receive a four-day VPN trial whose countdown starts only after their first successful VPN connection.

scope.in: TRIAL_DAYS default from 3 to 4, pending trial lifecycle after email verification, first-handshake activation from VPN stats, idempotent subscription update, subscription countdown API, dashboard remaining-time UX, and calendar highlighting active subscription dates.
scope.out: Deploy/install script changes, MTProto proxy expiration changes, paid subscription duration changes, automatic VPN client rotation, and production backup/restore work.

steps.step-1: Update runtime settings and documentation to make the default free trial duration four days while preserving env override behavior.
steps.step-2: Add nullable pending-trial activation fields and migration metadata without breaking existing subscriptions.
steps.step-3: Change verified-email onboarding to create an eligible pending trial instead of an already-running trial.
steps.step-4: Allow verified users with pending trial state to retrieve and use their first VPN config before the countdown starts.
steps.step-5: Activate pending trial atomically from the first successful VPN handshake observed by the scheduler or stats refresh path.
steps.step-6: Expose subscription state with pending_activation, activated_at, expires_at, remaining_seconds, remaining_days, remaining_hours, and remaining_minutes.
steps.step-7: Update dashboard and subscription pages to show compact remaining days/hours/minutes and pending-first-connection state.
steps.step-8: Add calendar view with active subscription date range highlighting and mobile-safe controls.
steps.step-9: Run billing/VPN/scheduler regression tests, frontend build, Phase-45 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and no deploy/install diff guard.

data-flow.flow-1: Email verification -> M-041 onboarding -> M-004 creates pending trial -> M-022 exposes first config path -> M-009 shows "starts after first VPN connection".
data-flow.flow-2: VPN client connects -> M-003 updates last_handshake_at -> M-008 detects first handshake -> M-004 sets started_at and expires_at = started_at + 4 days once.
data-flow.flow-3: Dashboard/subscription request -> M-004 returns server-time countdown -> M-009 renders remaining time and active date calendar.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Verified user does not connect today: pending trial remains unstarted, config is available, and UI explains that timer starts after first VPN connection.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Verified user connects once: scheduler observes first handshake, starts the four-day countdown, and subsequent handshakes do not extend it.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Paid user or already-started trial: handshake activation does nothing destructive and paid subscription expiry remains governed by billing/payment rules.

phase-gates.gate-1: New verified registrations create pending trial state without starting the countdown.
phase-gates.gate-2: Pending trial users can retrieve and use their first VPN config.
phase-gates.gate-3: First successful VPN handshake activates exactly one four-day trial window.
phase-gates.gate-4: Subscription API returns precise server-side remaining time and active date range.
phase-gates.gate-5: User dashboard and subscription page are compact on mobile and avoid stale "3 days" copy.
phase-gates.gate-6: Existing paid, complimentary, expired, and already-active subscriptions are not extended accidentally.

risks.risk-1: If pending trial is treated as inactive everywhere, users can be blocked from the first config and never start the trial.
risks.risk-2: If activation is not idempotent, repeated handshakes can extend trial unfairly.
risks.risk-3: Scheduler delay may make countdown appear pending briefly after first connection; UI must tolerate that state.
risks.risk-4: Legacy users with already-running trials need migration-safe behavior and no retroactive surprise extension unless explicitly defined.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_trial_activation.py tests/test_billing_service.py tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-45.xml docs/modules/M-063.xml docs/verification/V-M-063.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/requirements.xml docs/current-status.xml

Phase-45.evidence: 2026-06-01: Phase-45 backend regression suite passed outside sandbox due known aiosqlite sandbox hang: 35 passed. Frontend npm run build passed, Phase-45 UX smoke passed, py_compile passed for changed backend files, and deploy/install diff guard showed no changes.
